title: Crisp Venezuelan Guanciale Pasta
description: A floral, tender weeknight pasta that pairs smoky guanciale with fresh tomatoes and basil for a quick, satisfying meal.
instructions: Fill a mason jar with water, bring to a boil, and add salt.
Add the diced guanciale and cook until it releases fat and starts to crisp, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and diced onion, sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the uncooked pasta to the jar, submerge it fully with the boiling water, and stir to prevent sticking.
Close the mason jar lid, reduce heat to a simmer, and let the pasta cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, about 10 minutes.
While the pasta cooks, toss the cherry tomatoes in olive oil, salt, and pepper.
When the pasta is cooked, pour in the tomato mixture, sprinkle with grated parmesan, and stir well.
Finish with chopped basil, lemon zest if using, and a final sprinkle of black pepper, then serve hot.
